attacking_style: Not an attacker. Elite leadout specialist who delivers sprinters in final 100-200m with precise timing, regardless of positioning disadvantage earlier in finale.
team_tactics: Functions as final pilot in sprint trains across multiple teams (Deceuninck, UAE). Can execute textbook leadouts even without traditional train depth, using veteran positioning to compensate for team strength deficits.
terrain_preference: Flat sprint stages where final 500m positioning and timing are decisive. Thrives in technical finales requiring navigation from suboptimal positions to perfect delivery inside 200m.
vulnerability: Not a GC or breakaway rider. Limited value on mountain stages or reduced bunch sprints. Effectiveness depends entirely on having a sprinter to deliver.
key_patterns: Delivers sprinters perfectly inside 200m with superior timing across multiple teams (4 observations)
Compensates for weak sprint train infrastructure through veteran positioning skills (3 observations)
Executes textbook leadouts from disadvantaged positions deeper in peloton (2 observations)
Functions effectively as lone leadout when team lacks traditional train depth (2 observations)
form_trajectory: positive: Consistent elite-level leadout execution from 2018-2021 across teams, with 2021 Benelux showing continued dominance delivering Bennett twice
